data_window_note: MMS L2 data ~90 days behind real-time

unusual_events.explanation: Unusually large magnetic field: B_max = 34.6 nT (4.8σ above mean). Possible causes: magnetospheric compression, flux tube boundary, current sheet crossing, or reconnection inflow region.

Fast electron beam: mean V_e = 396.6 km/s > 300 km/s threshold. Ratio v_beam/v_th = 0.0964. When v_beam >> v_thermal, the electron velocity distribution function develops a positive slope df/dv > 0 at v_phase = ω_pe/k, which is the classical two-stream instability condition. Theory (Eq.18) predicts maximum growth rate γ_max at k_res = ω_pe/v_beam.

electron_holes.note: 9 bipolar E∥ events above 4.04 mV/m — consistent with nonlinear TSI saturation products (electron holes/phase-space vortices)

electrons.beam_note: ELECTRON BEAM — mean Ve=396.6 km/s > 300 km/s threshold. TSI conditions present.
electrons.dfdv_note: v_beam/v_th = 0.10 < 1.5 → no bump-on-tail. Beam insufficient for classical TSI.

theory_validation.validation_note: Theory predicts TSI active for v₀/c=0.0013: max γ = 0.35355 ωₚ at k=464.507. E-field doubles every 0.02 ms.

tsi_weibel_chain.note: Chain not detected — anisotropy too low (A=-0.073 < 0.05)

langmuir_proxy.note: f_pe=24.94 kHz outside EDP frequency range (Nyquist ~16 Hz for slow survey)
